title: echinocereus pectinatus var. neomexicanus
intro: Echinocereus pectinatus var. neomexicanus, also known as the New Mexican hedgehog cactus, is a type of succulent plant belonging to the Echinocereus genus. It is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of certain skin conditions. The herbal preparations of Echinocereus pectinatus var. neomexicanus include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by drying the plant's stems and flowers and then infusing them in hot water or oil.
